excluded: a claim that PhaseWrap replaces RoPE
a claim that target-attention supervision is standard free decoder generation
a claim that a one-seed diagnostic satisfies the five-seed promotion standard
supported: A target-attention-supervised diagnostic for the Stage 52/53 two-block decoder path.
Evidence separating attention-selection repair from learned value-output retrieval success.
Fair RoPE/ALiBI/sinusoidal/no-position/PhaseWrap comparisons with train/validation/test metrics and failed-run retention.

decision.claim_boundary: Target-attention supervision does not establish retrieval generalization or attention repair.

model.auxiliary_loss: target-position attention negative log likelihood on both attention blocks
model.trained_parameters: token embeddings, two q/k/v/o attention blocks, vocab output projection, positional scale
model.value_output_mode: learned vocab softmax, no fixed copy output
